methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $1.8B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Axiom Space, a Series C-stage space and aerospace company founded by Michael T. Suffredini and Kam Ghaffarian, carries a current post-money valuation of $2.5B following $850M+ in total capital raised. The private frontier-tech space comprises approximately 8 comparable private companies at similar stages, alongside 5 public-market comparables across the broader Space & Aerospace sector.

Two valuation methods triangulate the assessment. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.7x Series C step-up multiplier to the $2.5B last post-money, implying $4.2B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage private companies—Impulse Space ($1.8B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B), Skyryse ($1.1B), and Isar Aerospace ($1.0B)—yields a median of $1.3B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $1.8B. Public-comp anchoring using a 53.2x P/S multiple across 6 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) could not be applied due to the absence of disclosed revenue metrics for private peers.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $1.1B to $4.9B, with a median of $2.8B. The current $2.5B valuation sits near the lower bound of this range, suggesting modest upside to next-stage valuations under stage-progression logic but material upside relative to recent peer medians. Key uncertainty centers on the significance of Axiom's operational differentiation relative to the peer set, where valuations cluster substantially below the stage-progression implication.
